items.title: Tokenization could make finance faster, but also more susceptible to shocks, IMF says
items.description: Tokenization could make finance faster and cheaper. It also makes it more vulnerable to sudden shocks, the International Monetary Fund said.

items.title: Live updates: Bitcoin rises above $61,000 as the red hot semiconductor trade starts to fade
Binance says MiCA should be judged by who it licenses, not who it excludes
items.description: Europe head Gillian Lynch said Binance met Greece's licensing requirements and that the company remains committed to the EU, despite withdrawing its MiCA application days before the July 1 deadline.

items.title: Finally. $221 million flow into Bitcoin ETFs, ending a painful 10-day outflow streak
items.description: Spot ETFs had their strongest inflow day in two months, driven by funds other than BlackRock’s IBIT.

items.title: XRP bulls test path back toward $1.10 as token zips 4% higher
items.description: Buyers defended a higher low and pushed XRP toward near-term resistance, though the move still needs a clean break above $1.10 to confirm momentum.

items.title: Ether and solana extend gains as a short squeeze lifts bitcoin toward $62,000
items.description: Bearish traders lost $281 million in liquidations over 24 hours, nearly double the longs, as bitcoin pushed to its strongest level in two weeks. Ether is up almost 10% on the week and solana nearly 19% while a rebound in tech stocks eased the pressure from the AI trade.

items.title: Securitize tokenizes $295 million of its own stock on Solana and Avalanche amid NYSE debut
items.description: The newly-public company's shares are the largest issuer-sponsored tokenized stock at launch, aiming to make a point against rival third-party stock token issuers.

items.title: EToro invests in onchain derivatives platform Extended as brokers race into DeFi
items.description: The broker plans to bring perpetual futures into Zengo wallet and expand DeFi products to its core platform as rivals like Robinhood expand onchain offerings.

items.title: India's central bank revives push to isolate banks from crypto: Report
items.description: The Indian central bank reportedly urged lawmakers to keep banks insulated from crypto and private stablecoins while preserving room for regulated tokenization.

items.title: Irish authorities seize another 500 Bitcoin, bringing 2026 total to 1,500 BTC
items.description: Irish authorities recovered 500 Bitcoin in criminal proceeds, bringing the total seized by the Criminal Assets Bureau to about $92 million in BTC for 2026.

items.title: US spot Bitcoin ETFs top $200M in daily inflows for first time since May
items.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs post $221.7 million inflows, the strongest daily intake since early May, as Bitcoin recovers above $61,000.

items.title: Teen ‘Scattered Spider’ suspect extradited to US over $8M crypto ransom
items.description: Peter Stokes, 19, has been charged in the US for allegedly taking part in a hacking group’s unsuccessful $8 million crypto ransom scheme.

items.title: AI agent development hasn’t accelerated as expected, Zuckerberg says
items.description: Mark Zuckerberg’s comments came on the same day that Meta expanded its Meta Business Agent globally for businesses on Instagram, Messenger and WhatsApp.

items.title: US dominates Polymarket political bets despite geoblock: Report
items.description: New data from Allium suggests that US users are bypassing geoblocks to bet on markets offered on Polymarket’s global platform.

items.title: Strategy will be ‘less important’ in Bitcoin after STRC incident: Bitwise
items.description: Bitwise’s Matt Hougan said Strategy's STRC offer of high yields and low volatility was always a questionable fit for buying Bitcoin, as the cryptocurrency offers neither.

items.title: Securitize gains on NYSE debut with tokenized stocks live on Solana, Avalanche
items.description: Securitize has issued tokenized versions of its shares on Solana and Avalanche, marking the first time a newly public company has done so on its debut.

items.title: Oman walks a diplomatic tightrope over Strait of Hormuz fees, creating a ‘blind spot’ for markets
items.description: Oman is using strategic ambiguity as talks over possible Strait of Hormuz fees raise legal, diplomatic and oil market concerns.

items.title: Extreme heat wave threatens U.S. power grids and July 4 travel
items.description: The heat wave threatens to overwhelm U.S. grids and may force some to change their plans on one of the busiest travel weeks of the year.

items.title: Gold prices set for first weekly rise in a month as investors scale back Fed rate hike bets
items.description: The metal was boosted as investors scaled back their bets on a looming rate hike.

items.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
items.description: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.

items.title: Venezuela quake toll climbs to 2,595 as damage estimates mount after strongest tremor in a century
items.description: Death toll from twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela jumped to 2,595, as rescue operations continued after the country's strongest tremors in over a century.

items.title: Kuaishou shares jump after Tencent joins $2.8 billion raise for Kling AI subsidiary
items.description: Kuaishou shares rose as much as 6.89% at Friday's Hong Kong market open before paring gains to trade around 0.75% higher.

items.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
items.description: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

items.title: Rafael Nadal talks tennis prize money, his hotels, and what sports taught him about business
items.description: Rafael Nadal opened his fourth Zel hotel as he expands into hospitality, education and sports after retiring from tennis.

items.title: BTSE Group Launches BTSE Indonesia Through Joint Venture, Enters One of Asia’s Fastest-Growing Crypto Markets

items.description: Marianne Lake spent years as a contender for the CEO job at JPMorgan. Last week, she was abruptly axed

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t-T34NHJBvY! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: German tank supplier buys UK company that helped make Spitfires
items.description: Bavaria-based Renk acquires David Brown Defence in deal worth $200mn

items.title: What has America ever done for us?
items.description: Jazz? The moon landings? Gerrymandering? On this 250th anniversary, it seems only right to think about this question

items.title: Albania’s flamingos meet the Trump family bulldozers
items.description: Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump plan a luxury residential complex that locals say will threaten a natural reserve

items.title: Top official recuses herself from NHS Palantir contract decision after business links
items.description: MPs had raised concerns over Samantha Jones’s previous consulting work

items.title: Taking the measure of the UK as a financial service centre
items.description: Turning a data-fuelled quantitative international ranking project into a thing about London

items.title: Hamish Powell gives a botanist’s guide to London
items.description: Camellias in Chiswick, topiary in Islington, orchids in Kew: what to see where

items.title: Burnham’s council homes plan poses thorny delivery question
items.description: Rebuilding needed but proposals still lack detail on local control and funding within Labour’s fiscal constraints

items.title: Will Late-Night TV Work on YouTube?
items.description: Julian Shapiro-Barnum, best known for a social media series called “Recess Therapy,” has started an online-only late-night show.

items.title: Why One Global Shipping Choke Point Isn’t a Blueprint for Another
items.description: The Strait of Malacca may be a model for how Oman and Iran could collect fees in the Strait of Hormuz, but the differences between the waterways are vast.

items.title: The Hottest Stock Markets Lead to the Biggest Losses
items.description: Investor enthusiasm culminated in some of the worst cases of wealth destruction in the last 100 years, a long-running study shows.

items.title: Egg Producers to Pay $3.3 Million to Settle Federal Price-Fixing Lawsuit
items.description: The companies also agreed to donate more than 53 million eggs to food banks and nonprofits in 17 states that joined the federal government in the antitrust action.

items.title: Many Children Don’t Have ‘Trump Accounts.’ Some Are Missing Out on $1,000.
items.description: More than six million have been signed up for the investment accounts, which open for contributions July 4, but there are barriers to uptake.

items.title: To Reduce Electrical Grid Strain Amid Heat Wave, Data Centers Are Ordered to Use Backup Power
items.description: As triple-digit temperatures engulf much of the United States, the Trump administration wants grid managers to require the use of backup power that often goes unused.

items.title: What Private Credit Is, and Why Investors Are So Worried About It
items.description: Some say the industry expanded too quickly and extended loans to companies that won’t be able to pay them back.
